State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444


July 17, 1981


Furman B. Roberts
City Attorney
CITY OF ORANGE
P.O. Box 449
Orange, CA 92666

Re: Request for Informal Advice -
    Councilman Fred Barrera

Dear Mr. Roberts:

     Thank you for your recent request for informal advice
on behalf of Councilman Fred Barrera. As you have related
them to us, the material facts are as follows:

                            FACTS

     1. Councilman Barrera owns the site on the northwest
corner of West Chapman Avenue and Batavia Street. At that
site he operates a Union Oil Service Station.

     2. The City of Orange proposes to widen West Chapman
Avenue adjacent to Councilman Barrera's property.

     3. There are two alternative proposals for doing this,
which are illustrated on the map 039-045-30, which you
have provided to us. (Copy attached as Exhibit "A".)

     4. Under one proposal ("minimum take") a substantial
portion (8.5 feet) of Councilman Barrera's frontage on West
Chapman Avenue would be taken, leaving very little space
between the curb and the south island of pumps.

     5. Under the second proposal ("maximum take"), a great
portion (16.5 feet) of Councilman Barrera's frontage on West
Chapman Avenue would be taken, completely removing the
south island of pumps.

     6. The City Council will be considering all of these
matters.

Mr. Furman B. Roberts                    -2-                    July 17, 1981

        7.    Councilman Barrera's interest in his property
exceeds $1,000, and has been a source of income to him in
excess of $250 in the last 12 months.

                               QUESTION

        You have asked our advice as to whether the Political
Reform Act (Government Code Sections 81001-91014)1/ requires
Councilman Barrera to disqualify himself from participating
in these decisions.

                               ANALYSIS

        Section 87100 requires all public officials to dis-
qualify themselves from participating in any way in the making
of any governmental decision in which he has a financial interest.
Section 87103 defines what constitutes a financial interest and
includes:

        (a)  Any business entity in which the public official
has a direct or indirect investment worth more than
one thousand dollars ($1,000).

        (b)  Any real property in which the public official has
a direct or indirect investment worth more than one
thousand dollars ($1,000).

        (c)  Any source of income...aggregating two hundred
fifty dollars ($250) or more...to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is
made.

        Clearly, Councilman Barrera's financial interest in the
subject property meets all three of these tests. However,
Section 87103 also requires that the effect on such financial
interests be "material" and "reasonably foreseeable". Guid-
ance in this regard is given by the Commission's regulation,
2 Cal. Adm. Code §18702.2/

        Under our regulation, if the effect will be to increase
or decrease Councilman Barrera's gross revenues or net income
by at least $1,000,3/ or to increase or decrease the income

________________________

1/All references are to the Government Code.

2/Copy attached.

3/We assume that the applicable percentage tests in 18702
(b)(1)(a) and (b) are met. These assumptions appear appro-
priate based upon a review of the facts involved in our advice
letter of June 3, 1980, for Councilman Barrera (referred to
in your letter).

Mr. Furman B. Roberts                    -3-                    July 17, 1981


produced by his property by more than $50 per month,4/then
it is reasonably foreseeable that the financial effect
will be material. It seems safe to us to conclude that at
least one of these tests will be met. Furthermore, under
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18702(b)(3)(a), the effect of the
decision will almost assuredly directly decrease his income
by $100 or more.

                              CONCLUSION

     Therefore, we conclude that Councilman Barrera must
disqualify himself and may not "make, participate in making
or in any way attempt to use his official position to
influence" the decision of the City of Orange relative to
the widening of East Chapman Avenue in front of his property.

     Our regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700 5/
further defines what constitutes participating in a decision.
Subdivision (f) states:

     (f)  "In any way attempting to use his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision" shall
not include:
          (1) Appearances by a public official as a member
of the general public before an agency in the
course of its prescribed governmental function to
represent himself or herself on matters related
solely to his or her personal interest;

                                             (Emphasis added.)


     Therefore, so long as Councilman Barrera represents
himself on this matter solely related to his personal
interest (and not that of Union Oil Company), then he may
appear before the city agencies involved (but may not lobby
in private) on this issue.

                                             Sincerely,

                                             Robert E. Leidigh
                                             Counsel
                                             Legal Division
REL/gs

_________________________________

     4/Again, we assume that the 5% test is met since, under
the second proposal he will lose 50% of his pump islands.

     5/Copy attached.

orange civic center • 300 east chapman avenue • orange, california 92666
post office box 449

city attorney
(714) 532-0351

incorporated 1858

July 6, 1981

Fair Political Practices Commission
State of California
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca. 95804

Attn: Ms. Barbara A. Milman
Chief of Legal Division

Re: Request for Informal Advice Regarding Participation and
Voting on Issue of West Chapman Avenue Street Widening
by Councilman Fred Barrera

Gentlemen:

I have been requested by Councilman Fred Barrera, a
member of the City Council of the City of Orange, to inquire
of your agency whether he may participate in deliberations
and vote on the issue of widening a major arterial street
known as Chapman Avenue in the western portion of the City
from Main Street easterly to Clark Street. This project is
being constructed pursuant to a Federal Aid Urban project
participation project using Arterial Highway Funds derived
from taxes on gasoline sales. I include a street map describing the street to be widened and pointing out the
limits of the project. Also, I have indicated with an arrow
on that map the location of Councilman Barrera's property.

As you can see, Councilman Barrera owns the site on the
northwest corner of Chapman and Batavia at which site he
operates a Union Oil Service Station. Several years ago,
while Councilman Barrera was still a citizen of the City of
Orange and not a member of the City Council, the City
widened Batavia Street and took a portion of his property
located on the east side of his service station building.

Now, the City proposes to widen West Chapman from Main
Street to Clark Street on the east which portion of West
Chapman includes the service station site owned by Councilman Barrera.

Fair Political Practices Commission
July 6, 1981
Page two

Enclosed herein is a list of Objectives and Goals of
this F.A.U. project for West Chapman. Also, I enclose a
copy of a detailed map showing the alternatives for widening
West Chapman Avenue and how these alternatives would affect
Councilman Barrera's property. You will notice that if a
bus bay is installed on his property, the effect on Council-
man Barrera's property is quite severe since one of the
two remaining gasoline pump islands will be required to be
removed from his station. If the bus bay is not installed
at the point of Councilman Barrera's property, then the
effect would be less severe as shown by the minimum take
or green line. Whether to install this bus bay at this
particular location using his property is one of the deci-
sions which the Council may have to make.

You may safely assume that Councilman Barrera's in-
terest in his property exceeds one thousand dollars ($1,000)
and has been a source of income to him in excess of two
hundred and fifty dollars ($250) during the preceding 12
months.

Your office has previously rendered an opinion to me on
June 3, 1980, concerning Councilman Barrera in connection
with the J & L Oil Company matter.

Very truly yours,

Furman B. Roberts
City Attorney

ance in this regard is given by the Commission's regulation,

As you have related them to us, the material facts are as follows: FACTS 1. Councilman Barrera owns the site on the northwest corner of West Chapman Avenue and Batavia Street. At that site he operates a Union Oil Service Station. 2. The City of Orange proposes to widen West Chapman Avenue adjacent to Councilman Barrera's property. 3. There are two alternative proposals for doing this, which are illustrated on the map 039-045-30, which you have provided to us. (Copy attached as Exhibit "A".) 4. Under one proposal ("minimum take") a substantial portion (8.5 feet) of Councilman Barrera's frontage on West Chapman Avenue would be taken, leaving very little space between the curb and the south island of pumps. 5. Under the second proposal ("maximum take"), a great portion (16.5 feet) of Councilman Barrera's frontage on West Chapman Avenue would be taken, completely removing the south island of pumps. 6. The City Council will be considering all of these matters. Mr. Furman B. Roberts -2- July 17, 1981 7. Councilman Barrera's interest in his property exceeds $1,000, and has been a source of income to him in excess of $250 in the last 12 months. ANALYSIS Section 87100 requires all public officials to dis- qualify themselves from participating in any way in the making of any governmental decision in which he has a financial interest. Section 87103 defines what constitutes a financial interest and includes: (a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000). (b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth more than one thousand dollars ($1,000). (c) Any source of income...aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more...to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. Clearly, Councilman Barrera's financial interest in the subject property meets all three of these tests.
